channel.title: Github on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Github on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent for Linear is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub says Copilot cloud agent is now generally available for Linear. Users can assign Linear issues to the agent, which then analyzes the issue contents and works asynchronously in the background.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub MCP Server supports the next MCP specification
item.description:
GitHub MCP Server will support the upcoming MCP spec ahead of its official release. The key protocol change is a move to a stateless core on 28 July 2026.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Mobile: Fix failing Actions checks with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub Mobile now lets you ask Copilot coding agent to investigate and fix failing GitHub Actions checks on a pull request directly from the mobile app. The feature starts from a failed check and uses Copilot cloud agent to work on the issue.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Multi-select fields for Projects and Issues in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Projects and Issue fields now support multi-select fields in public preview, allowing a single field to store multiple values. This lets you tag an item with all relevant labels or categories instead of choosing just one.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Agent automation controls in GitHub Issues in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Issues now exposes the reason behind agent-driven changes and lets users review proposed automation actions before they are applied. The feature is in public preview.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: New Copilot usage metrics impact dashboard
item.description:
GitHub released a new Copilot metrics impact dashboard for enterprise administrators and organization owners. It is meant to help them report Copilot impact using usage metrics and related data.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming GHES change impacting uploading support bundles
item.description:
GitHub will begin rejecting some GHES support bundle uploads starting August 18, 2026 because of an upcoming security change. Administrators should review the new upload requirements before that date to avoid failed submissions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Gemini 3.6 Flash is now available in GitHub Copilot
item.description:
GitHub Copilot is rolling out Gemini 3.6 Flash, Google’s latest Flash model. GitHub says it is intended for web and app development, coding, and longer-horizon agentic tasks, with configurable behavior.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: AI credit pools for cost centers in the billing UI
item.description:
GitHub Billing UI now lets you manage a cost center’s AI credit pool directly when creating or editing cost centers. Before this change, that configuration was only available through another interface.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot users can now see AI credits used per billing cycle
item.description:
Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise users can now view how many AI credits they have used in the current billing cycle, even if they do not have an individual budget. The usage count is available in GitHub Copilot usage.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Code Quality is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub Code Quality is now generally available for GitHub Enterprise Cloud and GitHub Team. The announcement says it is intended to help with the growing volume of code produced with AI assistance.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Repository-level GitHub Copilot usage metrics generally available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics REST API now includes repository-level activity. Two new endpoints provide a daily per-repository breakdown of pull request activity for Copilot coding agent and Copilot code review.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot app now available in the usage metrics API
item.description:
The GitHub Copilot usage metrics API now includes GitHub Copilot app usage in enterprise and organization 1-day and 28-day reports. This adds admin visibility into Copilot app usage data.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot code review: Customization and configurability improvements
item.description:
Copilot code review now supports a firewall, custom setup steps, and independent runner configurations. It also reads custom instructions from the head branch to make testing and validation easier.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Mobile: Fix pull request comments with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub Mobile now lets you select “Fix with Copilot” from Copilot code review pull request comments. The action is available in the pull request’s main view and uses the Copilot cloud agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Advanced search for Projects is generally available
item.description:
GitHub Projects views now support advanced search in the filter bar. You can combine conditions with logical AND and OR to build more precise views without maintaining a separate setup.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Repository admins can archive pull requests
item.description:
Repository admins can archive pull requests to remove them from public view without deleting them. Archived pull requests are closed and locked.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: REST API endpoints for Visual Studio Subscription management
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise Cloud admins can now use REST API endpoints to manage Visual Studio Subscription (VSS) assignments programmatically. The new endpoint returns all VSS assignments for an enterprise, including assignment details.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Xcode 27 runner image now in public preview
item.description:
GitHub-hosted macOS runners now support Xcode 27 in public preview, letting you build and test Apple apps against the latest Xcode version. This is an early-access runner image release.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Improvements to secret scanning and public monitoring
item.description:
GitHub rolled out updates to secret scanning and public monitoring. Resend is now a secret scanning partner, and secret scanning can detect new secret types from APIclub.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio — June update
item.description:
June 2026’s Visual Studio Copilot update focuses on usage visibility, a new trust layer for MCP servers, and the first C++ scenarios. The post emphasizes controls and clarity rather than new broad functionality.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot for JetBrains expands BYOK capabilities
item.description:
GitHub Copilot for JetBrains now supports expanded bring-your-own-key (BYOK) customization across all Copilot tiers, with broader model provider flexibility and improved plugin/provider experiences. The update also includes changes to conversational interactions in the IDE.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Code scanning shows AI security detections on pull requests
item.description:
GitHub code scanning now shows AI-powered security detections on pull requests. The feature expands coverage to languages and frameworks not currently supported by CodeQL.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot version updates introduce default package cooldown
item.description:
Dependabot version updates now use a default package cooldown: it will wait at least three days after a new release appears in the registry before opening a version update pull request. This applies to version updates and delays PR creation for recently published releases.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Security reviews now available in the GitHub Copilot app
item.description:
GitHub Copilot app now supports running security reviews on in-flight code changes with the /security-review slash command. The feature is available in public preview.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Manage secret scanning custom patterns via REST API
item.description:
GitHub now supports managing secret scanning custom patterns through the REST API. The new generally available endpoints let security teams list, create, and edit custom patterns programmatically.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Code Quality license estimate in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Code Quality now shows the number of active committers for repositories across an enterprise, providing an estimate of Code Quality license cost before it is finalized. The feature is in public preview.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Separate SSO and Organizations pages in Settings
item.description:
GitHub split the combined SSO & Organizations page in user settings into two separate pages: SSO and Organizations. The change is intended to make these settings easier to find and manage.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: CodeQL 2.26.0 adds Kotlin 2.4.0 support and AI prompt injection detection
item.description:
CodeQL 2.26.0 adds support for Kotlin 2.4.0 and introduces detection for AI prompt injection issues in code scanning. The release updates the static analysis engine used by GitHub code scanning.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Clearer names for secret scanning detector types
item.description:
GitHub is renaming secret scanning detector types to make it clearer how each detector finds secrets. This is a naming-only change.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Per-user states for multi-user budgets in the REST API
item.description:
GitHub REST API now returns per-user progress for multi-user budgets from a single endpoint. This lets clients identify users nearing their limits without making separate requests.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Mobile: Improved filters and sorting for Copilot sessions
item.description:
GitHub Mobile now supports improved filtering and sorting for Copilot sessions. The update is meant to make it easier to find specific sessions as the list grows.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: New pull requests dashboard is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub’s refreshed pull requests dashboard is now generally available at github.com/pulls. It provides a single place to track, prioritize, and act on pull requests that need attention.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: OpenAI’s GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna are now available in GitHub Copilot
item.description:
GitHub Copilot is rolling out OpenAI’s GPT-5.6 family. The release adds three variants — Sol, Terra, and Luna — so users can choose a model based on the task.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Organization-level targeting for GitHub Code Quality
item.description:
GitHub Code Quality now supports organization-level targeting, letting owners enable or disable it for only selected repositories instead of the entire organization. This adds finer-grained control over rollout and removal.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Ask Copilot for a repository overview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot can now provide a high-level overview of a repository from the repository home page. The feature is meant to help users quickly understand repositories they are exploring for the first time.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Innersource security advisories are generally available
item.description:
GitHub Advanced Security enterprise customers can now publish internal security advisories for innersource repositories. These advisories work like open source advisories, but visibility is restricted to repositories owned by the enterprise.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprise-managed OpenTelemetry export for VS Code and CLI
item.description:
GitHub Copilot now supports enterprise-managed OpenTelemetry export for VS Code and CLI, letting organizations direct telemetry to an approved collector centrally instead of requiring developers to set OTEL_* environment variables. The configuration is delivered through enterprise-managed settings.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Deploy managed Copilot settings via MDM in VS Code and CLI
item.description:
Enterprise admins can now push managed GitHub Copilot settings to devices using native MDM or file-based configuration, in addition to the existing server-managed channel. The update applies to VS Code and the CLI.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code, June 2026 releases
item.description:
This changelog covers GitHub Copilot updates in Visual Studio Code across VS Code v1.123 through v1.127, released during June and early July 2026. The provided excerpt does not include specific feature or bug-fix details.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: setup-java v5.5.0: signature verification, Kona JDK, and Maven fixes
item.description:
setup-java v5.5.0 adds cryptographic signature verification for downloaded JDKs, support for Kona JDK, and several Maven-related fixes. It is the release over v5.4.0.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: npm install-time security and GAT bypass2fa deprecation
item.description:
npm v12 is now generally available and tagged latest. It enables the install-time security defaults announced in June and starts deprecating GAT bypass2fa.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Mobile: Fix merge conflicts with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub Mobile now lets you fix pull request merge conflicts using the Copilot cloud agent. This makes it possible to unblock PRs from mobile while away from a desktop.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Mobile: Live notifications for Copilot CLI sessions
item.description:
GitHub Mobile now supports live coding agent notifications for remote Copilot CLI sessions. This lets users follow agent activity that starts outside the app.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Add review cycles and time to adoption phases in the usage API
item.description:
The Copilot usage metrics API adds two review-velocity fields for each AI adoption phase in enterprise and organization reports. The new data extends the adoption phase cohort fields with review cycles and time in phase.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Codex as agent provider and agentic enhancements in JetBrains IDEs
item.description:
GitHub added Codex as a new agent provider in public preview. JetBrains IDE updates also include Hooks support in the Customizations editor, richer MCP server management, and custom model support via configuration.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Kimi K2.7 now available for Copilot Business and Enterprise
item.description:
Kimi K2.7 is now available on GitHub Copilot Business and Enterprise. This expands availability beyond the Copilot Pro, Pro+, and Max plans announced on July 1, 2026.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Per-user budgets for cost centers in the billing UI
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise Cloud now supports creating per-user budgets for cost centers directly in the billing UI. Enterprise admins can manage these user-level budgets alongside cost centers and budgets in one place.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning extended metadata and multipart validation
item.description:
GitHub secret scanning now surfaces extended metadata for supported secret types to help identify ownership and impact of leaked secrets. The update also includes general availability of extended metadata checks and multipart validation support.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Restrict who can dismiss reviews in rulesets
item.description:
GitHub repository rulesets now support restricting who can dismiss pull request reviews. The feature is generally available and lets you control which users can clear review requirements.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot app available to all
item.description:
The GitHub Copilot app is now available on all Copilot plans. Users can sign in with a GitHub account and use it for agent-driven development from desktop on macOS, Windows, and Linux.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Billing Preview app will be retired on August 3
item.description:
GitHub will retire the Copilot Billing Preview app on August 3, 2026. Users who rely on it for Copilot spend review are directed to use built-in GitHub visibility instead.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Improved accuracy and coverage in Copilot usage metrics reports
item.description:
GitHub improved the Copilot usage metrics API to make reports more complete and accurate. The update adds support for Copilot CLI suggested lines of code and includes users that were previously only partially captured.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of Gemini 2.5 Pro and Gemini 3 Flash
item.description:
GitHub will deprecate Gemini 2.5 Pro and Gemini 3 Flash across all Copilot experiences on July 31. This includes Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot CLI no longer needs a personal access token in GitHub Actions
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI can now run in GitHub Actions with the built-in GITHUB_TOKEN. A personal access token is no longer required for this workflow.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot agent session streaming is now in public preview
item.description:
GitHub has put Copilot agent session streaming into public preview for Enterprise Cloud customers with enterprise managed users. Session data is available across Copilot clients, including cloud agents on github.com and data-resident deployments.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Cost centers now support AI credit pools
item.description:
Cost centers can now be assigned caps on how much of an enterprise’s monthly included AI credits they can consume. The feature is available through the REST API.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Issue fields are now generally available
item.description:
Issue fields are now generally available for all GitHub organizations on Free, Team, Enterprise, and GitHub Enterprise Cloud with data residency plans. Support will also ship in GitHub Enterprise Server 3.23.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning public monitoring for enterprises
item.description:
GitHub announced secret scanning public monitoring for enterprises, aimed at helping organizations detect and respond to secret leaks as soon as they appear publicly. The post is a brief announcement and does not include implementation details in the provided text.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprises can default to auto model selection
item.description:
Enterprise admins can now set model: auto in enterprise-managed managed-settings.json so GitHub Copilot uses auto model selection by default for new conversations. This setting is added in .github-private/.github/copilot/managed-settings.json for enterprise-managed configuration.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Models is being fully retired on July 30, 2026
item.description:
GitHub Models will be fully retired on July 30, 2026. GitHub had already announced in June that it was closing the service to new customers.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprise managed-settings.json is generally available
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise Cloud now supports AI standards configuration through a managed-settings.json file stored in a .github-private repository within a selected organization. This gives enterprises a centralized way to define governance and related settings.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning adds validators for Asana, IBM, and MessageBird
item.description:
GitHub secret scanning now validates Asana, IBM, and MessageBird secrets to help determine whether detected credentials are still active. The update adds validity checks for these secret types.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: New C++ language server config skill for Copilot CLI
item.description:
The Microsoft C++ Language Server is now available as a Copilot Plugins marketplace plugin. It includes a built-in setup skill for Copilot CLI to automate project setup.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Kimi K2.7 Code is generally available in GitHub Copilot
item.description:
Kimi K2.7 Code is now generally available in GitHub Copilot. It is the first open-weight model available as a selectable option in the Copilot model picker.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot vision is generally available
item.description:
Copilot Vision is now generally available. You can attach images and PDFs to chat prompts so Copilot can use them alongside your code when responding.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Set AI credit session limits in Copilot CLI and SDK
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI and the GitHub Copilot SDK now support AI credit session limits, letting you cap how much an agent can spend in a single session. This is intended to help control usage during longer-running agent workflows.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Browser tools for GitHub Copilot in VS Code are generally available
item.description:
Browser tools for GitHub Copilot in VS Code are now generally available. GitHub also clarified which permissions remain under user control and noted that existing network domain controls still apply.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Claude Sonnet 5 is generally available for GitHub Copilot
item.description:
Claude Sonnet 5 is now generally available in GitHub Copilot. GitHub says it offers strong coding performance for everyday development and agentic workflows as a new Sonnet-class model option.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub code coverage merge protection for pull requests
item.description:
GitHub added branch ruleset support to block pull request merges when code coverage falls below a threshold you configure. You can set coverage-based merge requirements to enforce a minimum percentage and limit coverage decreases.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Releases: Sidebar navigation and per-asset download counts
item.description:
GitHub Releases now include a dedicated sidebar table of contents for easier navigation, and release metadata has been rearranged for a more consistent layout. Per-asset download counts are also shown on release pages.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Agent is now available in JetBrains AI Assistant
item.description:
GitHub and JetBrains announced a deeper integration between JetBrains AI Assistant and GitHub Copilot. Copilot Agent is now available inside JetBrains AI Assistant.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Open source license compliance is in public preview
item.description:
GitHub has put open source license compliance into public preview for enterprises. The feature adds centralized, ruleset-based checks to enforce dependency license policy and block noncompliant licenses.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot no longer infers .npmrc
item.description:
Dependabot no longer infers .npmrc settings for npm private registries. Instead of reconstructing .npmrc from lockfile URLs, it will stop trying to guess configuration based on potentially incorrect lockfile data.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming cloud data retention policy for closed security alerts
item.description:
GitHub will begin enforcing a cloud data retention policy for closed Dependabot security alerts on August 25, 2026. The post says this provides a defined retention period for alert data, but it does not include the exact duration in the excerpt provided.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Per-user AI credit budgets available for cost centers
item.description:
Enterprise admins can now assign a per-user AI credit budget to a cost center, with the budget applying to each individual member. The budget updates as cost center membership changes.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Claude Opus 4.8 (fast mode) is now in preview for GitHub Copilot
item.description:
GitHub Copilot is rolling out Claude Opus 4.8 in preview with fast mode enabled. GitHub says fast mode increases output token speed while keeping the same model intelligence.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Restrict issue creation to collaborators only
item.description:
Repository admins can now limit issue creation to collaborators with write access. This adds control over who can open issues and helps reduce unwanted issue creation.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Track total merges by adoption phase in enterprise and organization reports
item.description:
GitHub enterprise and organization reports now include the total number of pull requests merged by each AI adoption phase cohort. This extends the adoption phase metrics previously added to the Copilot usage metrics API.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Read-only Actions cache for untrusted triggers
item.description:
GitHub Actions now uses read-only cache tokens on the default branch for workflow events that can be triggered without repository write permissions. This narrows cache access for untrusted triggers by applying least privilege.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: MAI-Code-1-Flash for Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise
item.description:
MAI-Code-1-Flash, Microsoft AI’s in-house coding model, is now generally available for GitHub Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise. It builds on its recent expansion across Copilot surfaces and is described as purpose-built for coding and optimized.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Desktop 3.6: Worktrees and deeper Copilot integration
item.description:
GitHub Desktop 3.6 adds Git worktree support and expands GitHub Copilot integration for commit authoring and merge conflict resolution. It brings more of the Git workflow into Desktop.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot code review: Analysis depth and efficiency updates
item.description:
Copilot code review now uses built-in file exploration tools from the Copilot CLI and SDK. GitHub says this improves review cost efficiency without changing the existing workflow.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprise-managed settings now support strictKnownMarketplaces in VS Code and GitHub Copilot CLI
item.description:
GitHub added enterprise-managed support for strictKnownMarketplaces in VS Code and GitHub Copilot CLI. The setting is in public preview and lets enterprises control which plugins users can install.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Saved views for repository issues – Public Preview and adjustable row heights in projects
item.description:
GitHub added saved views to repository Issues pages in public preview, allowing teams to create and share filtered issue views. The release also includes adjustable row heights in Projects.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: More control over your GitHub-hosted runners
item.description:
GitHub Actions org admins can now control access to GitHub-hosted runners more tightly. They can disable standard hosted-runner labels like ubuntu-latest and add custom labels for finer-grained usage control.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Actions steps can now be run in parallel
item.description:
GitHub Actions now supports running workflow steps concurrently by running them in the background. Previously, steps always executed sequentially, with each step waiting for the prior one to finish.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: npm adds preventive account protection for high-impact accounts
item.description:
npm now applies a temporary preventive safeguard to high-impact accounts when it detects sensitive account changes. The measure is intended to reduce account-takeover risk for maintainers of widely used packages.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Red Hat Enterprise Linux runner images are now in public preview
item.description:
GitHub-hosted larger runners now support Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 and 10 images in public preview. The images were introduced in partnership with Red Hat and are available for organizations to use.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot for Jira is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot for Jira is now generally available after a public preview that began in March 2026. GitHub says it has added several improvements since preview, including model selection.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Cost centers now support enterprise teams
item.description:
GitHub cost centers now support adding enterprise teams as resources, so usage can be attributed according to your team structure. This update extends cost attribution for enterprises beyond individual resources.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Self-service credential revocation for incident response
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise owners can now use a new break-glass capability to immediately revoke all credentials for a compromised account or stolen credentials during incident response. The feature is self-service and intended for urgent security handling.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning adds extended metadata for Replicate secrets
item.description:
GitHub Secret Scanning now adds extended metadata when it detects Replicate secrets, giving more context about leaked credentials. The change applies to the Replicate secret pattern and is meant to improve incident response and triage.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Fetch Code Quality findings via REST API
item.description:
GitHub has added repository-level REST APIs for Code Quality findings in public preview. The new read-only endpoints expose code quality data via API, bringing parity closer to the GitHub UI.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Automatic Dependabot access to GitHub-hosted registries
item.description:
Dependabot can now read from private GitHub Packages registries without a personal access token when the package has granted repository access through “Manage Actions access.” This removes the need to configure a PAT for that dependency source.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot CLI: New terminal interface is generally available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI’s redesigned terminal interface is now generally available. It adds a tabbed layout for working with GitHub directly from the terminal.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Deprecation of Python 3.9 for Dependabot
item.description:
Dependabot no longer supports Python 3.9 because it has reached end of life. Continuing to use Python 3.9 may prevent Dependabot from creating pull requests.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot app support for BYOK
item.description:
GitHub Copilot app now supports bring your own key (BYOK), allowing agent sessions to use customer-managed model providers. Supported providers include OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, Microsoft Foundry, Anthropic, and more.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: New features and Claude as agent provider preview in JetBrains IDEs
item.description:
GitHub added support for organization and enterprise agents in JetBrains IDEs, along with Copilot CLI message queueing/steering and a new agent debug logs summary view. The update also previews Claude as an agent provider in JetBrains IDEs.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: AI credits consumed per user now in the Copilot usage metrics API
item.description:
The Copilot usage metrics API now includes per-user AI credits consumed per day. The new data is derived from the same AI credits consumption source used by the usage-based billing API.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of Opus 4.6 (fast)
item.description:
GitHub will deprecate Opus 4.6 (fast) across all Copilot experiences on June 29, 2026. This includes Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: MAI-Code-1-Flash available on more Copilot surfaces
item.description:
MAI-Code-1-Flash is now available in additional GitHub Copilot surfaces. The model can be used in Copilot CLI, the GitHub Copilot app, Copilot Chat on GitHub, and Visual Studio Code.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot code review: AGENTS.md support and UI improvements
item.description:
Copilot code review now reads repository-level AGENTS.md files and adds a Request button to make it easier to ask Copilot for a review on draft pull requests. GitHub says these updates are generally available.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Detecting Duplicate Issues – Public Preview and issue fields MCP support for GitHub Issues
item.description:
GitHub Issues adds duplicate issue detection in public preview and expands MCP support for issue fields. The change is aimed at reducing duplicate triage work for maintainers, especially in large repositories.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot-authored pull requests now included in author searches
item.description:
Pull request searches using the author: qualifier now include pull requests opened by the Copilot cloud agent on a user’s behalf. For example, author:@me on GitHub pull requests will return those Copilot-authored PRs as well.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Repository switcher generally available in global navigation
item.description:
GitHub’s repository switcher in global navigation is now generally available. It lets you jump between repositories without leaving the current page.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Actions: Build custom images from custom images
item.description:
GitHub Actions custom images now support building one custom image from another. This adds flexibility for composing and managing image-generation pipelines for GitHub-hosted runners.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Safer pull_request_target defaults for GitHub Actions checkout
item.description:
GitHub is changing the default behavior of the Actions checkout action for workflows triggered by pull_request_target to reduce common security mistakes. The goal is to avoid exposing base-branch credentials or secrets to untrusted pull request code.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Control who and what triggers GitHub Actions workflows
item.description:
GitHub Actions now has workflow execution protections in public preview for GitHub Enterprise, organizations, and repositories. Administrators can define allow lists to control which users or events can trigger workflows.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Generated release notes credit you for Copilot pull requests
item.description:
Generated release notes now credit Copilot pull requests in the list of merged pull requests since the last release. This affects the automatically generated release notes output.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Read remote repository content with GitHub CLI
item.description:
GitHub CLI now includes two commands for reading repository content without cloning: gh repo read-file and gh repo read-dir. These let you inspect remote files and directories directly from the terminal.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning updates – June 2026
item.description:
GitHub added secret scanning coverage in June 2026 with new partner integrations, more default push-protection blocks, additional validity checks, and richer metadata for leaked secrets.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprise-managed settings now support bypass permission controls
item.description:
Enterprise administrators can now set disableBypassPermissionsMode to "disable" in enterprise-managed settings.json to prevent GitHub Copilot CLI and VS Code from bypassing permission controls.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Auto mode in Copilot Chat available for all users
item.description:
GitHub Copilot auto model selection is now generally available in Copilot Chat on github.com and the GitHub mobile app for all Copilot plans. In auto mode, Copilot selects the model for the chat request.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Limit open pull requests for users without write access
item.description:
GitHub is adding a limit on how many open pull requests users without write access can have in a repository. The change is intended to reduce repeated low-quality or drive-by PRs and ease triage load for maintainers.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Agent finder for GitHub Copilot now available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot now includes agent finder, a feature for selecting which MCP servers, skills, canvases, agents, and tools an agent should use. GitHub says it reduces the need to manually wire these components and helps avoid filling the context window.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot individual plan sign-ups are reopening
item.description:
GitHub is gradually reopening sign-ups for Copilot Individual plans, including Student, Pro, Pro+, and Max, for new subscribers over the next couple of weeks.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot app generally available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot app is now generally available on macOS, Windows, and Linux. It serves as a native desktop app for agent-driven development on GitHub.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Models is no longer available to new customers
item.description:
GitHub is retiring GitHub Models. As an initial step, new customers can no longer use it, while existing organizations or enterprises that have already used it are not affected by this announcement.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Code Quality generally available July 20, 2026
item.description:
GitHub Code Quality, currently in public preview, will reach general availability on July 20, 2026. The preview has been used by enterprises to detect maintainability and reliability issues, enforce quality gates, and track code coverage.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Organization-level enablement for GitHub Code Quality
item.description:
GitHub Code Quality can now be enabled or disabled at the organization level with one toggle. This replaces the need to configure the feature repository by repository.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now include more of your active users
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics now use server-side telemetry in addition to client-side signals, so reports include more active users. The change affects enterprise usage reports and improves coverage of user activity.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot code review: New configurations and controls
item.description:
GitHub added new ways to configure Copilot code review, including organization runner controls, support for Copilot content exclusion, and removal of the repository custom instructions character limit.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Actions: Minimum version enforcement timeline for self-hosted runners
item.description:
GitHub Actions is resuming enforcement of minimum version requirements for self-hosted runners on github.com and GitHub Enterprise Cloud with Data Residency. The post says this is part of a broader effort to rebuild enforcement, but the excerpt does not include the specific minimum versions or timeline details.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Enterprise Server 3.21 is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise Server 3.21 is now generally available. The release adds improvements in deployment efficiency, monitoring, code security, and policy management, including organization custom properties now generally available.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Bot-created pull requests can run workflows if approved
item.description:
Pull requests created by github-actions[bot] can now run CI/CD workflows after a user approves them. The approval step is required as a security check before generated code is allowed to trigger workflows.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: AI usage report updates
item.description:
AI usage reports now include GitHub AI Credits usage in the standard report fields. To track AI credit consumption, use the quantity field for AI credit quantity and gross_amount for the associated amount.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot CLI: Configure everything from one place with /settings
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI adds a unified /settings command for managing configuration in one place. It consolidates older commands like /theme, /streamer-mode, and /experimental, along with other previously separate options.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: New runner images in public preview
item.description:
GitHub has added two new GitHub-hosted runner images for Actions in public preview. They are available to all users so workflows can be tested on the latest platforms before general release.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Agentic Workflows is now in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Agentic Workflows is now in public preview. It lets you automate reasoning-based tasks such as issue triage, CI failure analysis, and documentation updates using coding agents inside GitHub.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Agentic workflows no longer need a personal access token
item.description:
GitHub Agentic Workflows can now use the built-in GitHub Actions GITHUB_TOKEN instead of requiring a personal access token. This removes the need to create and store a PAT for these workflows.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: List, view, and create discussions in GitHub CLI
item.description:
GitHub CLI now includes a new gh discussion command group for GitHub Discussions. It lets you list, view, create, and update discussions from the CLI.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Manage sub-issues, types, and dependencies from GitHub CLI
item.description:
GitHub CLI now supports managing issue types, parent/sub-issue relationships, and issue dependencies directly from the terminal. This lets you structure and track issues without using the browser.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Chat now sees your agent sessions
item.description:
GitHub Copilot Chat on the web now has a better handoff to Copilot cloud agent sessions. It also adds the ability to search and query past agent sessions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprises can now create up to 500 cost centers
item.description:
GitHub has doubled the enterprise cost center limit from 250 to 500. This applies to enterprises managing many business units, departments, or products.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Incremental analysis for Go, C/C++, and CodeQL CLI
item.description:
CodeQL pull request scans for C/C++ and Go now use incremental analysis, which makes them faster. GitHub says this builds on earlier incremental analysis improvements for other languages and CodeQL CLI.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dedicated security review command now available in Copilot CLI
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI now includes an experimental /security-review slash command for running security reviews on code changes directly in the CLI. The feature is in public preview.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot version updates now support the Deno ecosystem
item.description:
Dependabot version updates now support Deno. The support is for version updates only, not security updates.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming breaking changes for npm v12
item.description:
npm v12 will change the default behavior of npm install with security-related defaults. The post says these changes are already available as warnings in npm 11.16.0 and newer.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Claude Fable 5 is generally available for GitHub Copilot
item.description:
Claude Fable 5 from Anthropic is now generally available in GitHub Copilot. GitHub describes it as a Mythos-class model aimed at long-horizon, autonomous coding and knowledge-work tasks.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Periodic code scanning of inactive repositories
item.description:
GitHub code scanning now supports scheduled security scans for repositories with no pushes or pull requests for six months or longer. This helps organizations keep inactive repositories under periodic review.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GPT-4.1 deprecated
item.description:
GitHub deprecated GPT-4.1 in all GitHub Copilot experiences on June 1, 2026. The affected surface areas include Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Expanded technical preview availability for the GitHub Copilot app
item.description:
GitHub has expanded the technical preview of the Copilot app to all existing Copilot Pro, Pro+, Business, and Enterprise customers. The app is available for download on Windows, macOS, and Linux.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot SDK is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot SDK is now generally available. It provides a stable API and production-ready support for embedding Copilot’s agentic engine into applications, services, and developer tools.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot CLI: Improved UI, rubber duck, prompt scheduling, and voice input
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI is getting a UI refresh, with a new experimental terminal interface and support for tabs. Rubber duck, prompt scheduling, and voice input are generally available.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Cloud and local sandboxes for GitHub Copilot now in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot now supports secure, isolated sandboxes for tool execution in both local and cloud environments, available in public preview. The update is intended to isolate Copilot actions from the rest of the system.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot code review for Azure Repos is now in technical preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot code review for Azure Repos is in technical preview, adding on-demand pull request reviews inside Azure DevOps. It must be enabled before use.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Shape Copilot code review around your team
item.description:
GitHub is previewing updates to Copilot code review that let it adapt to a team’s tools and standards, and vary review depth based on change complexity. The post says two public previews are shipping on a unified agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Extend GitHub with agent apps
item.description:
GitHub now supports “agent apps,” which are AI agents from GitHub partners that can be installed from the GitHub Marketplace and used directly in GitHub. The changelog note says they install like other GitHub Apps.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Introducing Copilot CLI and agentic capabilities enhancements in JetBrains IDEs
item.description:
GitHub added Copilot CLI support to Copilot for JetBrains IDEs and expanded agentic capabilities in the IDE integration. The update focuses on new Copilot CLI session features plus additional workflow improvements.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Gemini models in Copilot CLI, cloud agent, and the Copilot app
item.description:
GitHub added Gemini 3.1 Pro (Preview) and Gemini 3.5 Flash to more Copilot surfaces. The models are now available in Copilot CLI, Copilot cloud agent, and the GitHub Copilot app.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Eclipse: BYOK, skills, and chat updates
item.description:
GitHub Copilot in Eclipse adds a refreshed chat experience, more session context visibility, BYOK for Business and Enterprise, improved ABAP support, and skills-related updates. The changelog notes multiple chat and workflow changes, but the provided excerpt does not include full technical details.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Evaluation models in auto for individual plans
item.description:
GitHub Copilot now includes evaluation models for individual non-enterprise users, and these models may be selected by Copilot auto model selection. Users can disable evaluation-model usage in Copilot.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Updates to GitHub Copilot billing and plans
item.description:
GitHub Copilot now uses usage-based billing for all users. Copilot code review also consumes GitHub Actions minutes, in addition to GitHub billing.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics API adds cohorts for AI adoption
item.description:
GitHub’s Copilot usage metrics API now classifies engaged users into AI adoption cohorts, giving teams a more detailed view of how Copilot is being used. The change is intended to help measure adoption beyond simple activity counts.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Hard budget limits now available for GitHub Advanced Security
item.description:
GitHub Advanced Security now supports hard budget limits for GHAS SKUs, allowing enterprise admins and billing managers to prevent teams from exceeding allocated license budgets. This applies to license-based GHAS products.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: CodeQL 2.25.5 improves query accuracy for GitHub Actions
item.description:
CodeQL 2.25.5 was released with query accuracy improvements for GitHub Actions, along with additional accuracy improvements across C/C++.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Claude Opus 4.8 is generally available for GitHub Copilot
item.description:
Claude Opus 4.8 is now generally available in GitHub Copilot. GitHub says early testing showed improved code understanding and generation compared with earlier models.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Memory has more controls for deletion, scope, and the Copilot CLI
item.description:
GitHub updated Copilot Memory with better deletion controls, a repository-level disable option, and additional memory controls in Copilot CLI. The feature remains in public preview.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Code Quality: Repository Enablement API
item.description:
GitHub Code Quality now has a Repository Enablement API in public preview. It adds two new endpoints to programmatically enable and configure Code Quality on individual repositories.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Target Copilot models to organizations with model rules
item.description:
GitHub added targeted Copilot model rules for enterprise owners. This lets them control which Copilot models are available to each organization, instead of applying the same model availability across the whole enterprise.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot version updates now support the sbt ecosystem
item.description:
Dependabot version updates now support the sbt ecosystem. You can add sbt to dependabot.yml, and Dependabot will monitor build.sbt inputs and open pull requests when newer commits are available.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Filter secret scanning approval requests by sort order and bypass status
item.description:
GitHub is adding two delegated workflow improvements for secret scanning: you can now sort bypass and dismissal requests in the UI and filter approval requests by bypass status. The update is part of the secret scanning approval request workflow.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Classroom sign-ups are no longer available
item.description:
GitHub Classroom no longer accepts new sign-ups as of today. Existing accounts and classrooms can continue to be used while GitHub transitions to partner solutions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Code coverage on pull requests is now in public preview
item.description:
Code coverage metrics are now in public preview for GitHub Code Quality users on github.com. Pull requests now show an aggregate code coverage percentage directly in the PR view.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Staged publishing and new install-time controls for npm
item.description:
GitHub announced two npm supply-chain security updates: staged publishing is now generally available, and npm install gets new source अनुमति flags for file, remote, and directory dependencies. These new flags extend the existing –allow-git control.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot for Eclipse is open source
item.description:
GitHub Copilot for Eclipse is now open source. The code is available on GitHub under the MIT license.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Issue fields are now in public preview for all organizations
item.description:
GitHub has made issue fields available in public preview for all organizations on github.com and GitHub Enterprise Cloud with data residency. The feature lets teams define typed metadata on issues, such as Priority and Effort.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics reports now use GitHub-owned download URLs
item.description:
Copilot usage metrics report downloads now use a GitHub-owned custom domain instead of Azure Front Door URLs. GitHub says this improves URL stability.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Updates to available models in Copilot on web
item.description:
GitHub updated the model selection available for Copilot Chat on the web to improve consistency and response quality. The post says model choice is being limited, but the provided excerpt does not include the full list of changes.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Auto model selection now routes based on your task in VS Code
item.description:
GitHub Copilot auto model selection in VS Code now routes tasks to the best model based on utilization and model health metrics. GitHub says this is intended to improve quality, reliability, and token efficiency.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Semantic issue search in Copilot Chat
item.description:
GitHub Copilot Chat on the web now supports natural-language issue search, grouping, and analysis using a new semantic issues index. Results are context-aware and intended to help users find relevant issues more quickly.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Easily apply Copilot code review feedback with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub renamed Copilot code review’s “Implement suggestion” button to “Fix with Copilot”. The updated flow now includes a UI dialog for more control when applying suggestions with the Copilot cloud agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of Python 3.9 for Dependabot
item.description:
Dependabot will stop supporting Python 3.9 on June 23, 2026 because Python 3.9 has reached end of life. Users still on 3.9 may see Dependabot issues after the cutoff.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Gemini 3.5 Flash is generally available for GitHub Copilot
item.description:
Gemini 3.5 Flash is now generally available in GitHub Copilot. GitHub says it is rolling out broadly and positions it as a Flash-tier model with near-Pro coding quality, but the note does not include technical details beyond that.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Expanded OIDC support for Dependabot and code scanning
item.description:
Dependabot and code scanning now support OpenID Connect (OIDC) authentication for organization-level private registries on Cloudsmith and Google Artifact Registry.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Start a GitHub Advanced Security trial from a risk assessment
item.description:
Eligible enterprise admins can start a GitHub Advanced Security trial directly from a Secret Protection or Code Security risk assessment. This lets them begin evaluating the products without leaving the assessment flow.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Removal of code_scanning_upload field from rate_limit API endpoint
item.description:
GitHub removed the code_scanning_upload field from the REST API rate_limit endpoint response as of May 19, 2026. The field no longer appears in the resources object.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: One-click fixes for failing Actions with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub Actions failures can now be handed to Copilot cloud agent with one click for Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise subscribers. The new “Fix with Copilot” button starts an automated fix flow for failed jobs.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent: Fast, cost-efficient models for simple tasks
item.description:
GitHub Copilot cloud agent now supports additional models, letting users choose fast, cost-efficient options for simpler tasks. The change expands the set of models available when delegating work to the cloud agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Ask questions in context with Copilot on web
item.description:
Copilot chat on the web now opens directly on the page you’re viewing, so you can ask questions in context with less switching between pages.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Audit repository Copilot cloud agent configuration via the REST API
item.description:
GitHub added a public preview REST API to get a repository’s Copilot cloud agent configuration. This lets you audit that configuration programmatically.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Spaces API now generally available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot Spaces API is now generally available. It supports programmatic create, read, update, and delete operations for Spaces from external applications.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Remote control for Copilot CLI sessions now generally available on mobile, web, and VS Code
item.description:
Remote control for Copilot CLI sessions is now generally available on GitHub Mobile and github.com, with support also referenced for VS Code. It lets you start work in the terminal and continue managing the session from mobile or web.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GPT-5.3-Codex is now the base model for Copilot Business and Enterprise
item.description:
GPT-5.3-Codex is now the default base model for all Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise organizations, replacing GPT-4.1. GitHub says the base model is used when an organization has not selected another model.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub App installation tokens: Per-request override header
item.description:
GitHub is rolling out a new GitHub App installation token format and is providing a per-request override header so developers can validate apps and workflows before the rollout completes.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Grok Code Fast 1 deprecated
item.description:
GitHub deprecated Grok Code Fast 1 across all GitHub Copilot experiences on May 15, 2026. The deprecation applies to Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Memory supports user preferences for Pro, Pro+ users
item.description:
Copilot Memory now supports user-level preferences in early access for Copilot Pro and Pro+ users. Copilot can store stated or inferred personal preferences about how you like to work.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Timestamp fields in GitHub Projects
item.description:
GitHub Projects now has three built-in timestamp fields: Created, Updated, and Closed. These fields can be added to any project view for sorting and filtering work by when items were created, last updated, or closed.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Team-level Copilot usage metrics now available via API
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics API now includes a new user-teams report that maps each Copilot-licensed user to the teams they belong to. This can be joined with existing usage reports to analyze Copilot usage at the team level.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Actions: Upcoming image migrations
item.description:
GitHub Actions has announced two upcoming hosted runner image migrations. GitHub is also transitioning to own and maintain the Arm64 images for hosted runners.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot app is now available in technical preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot app is available in technical preview as a GitHub-native desktop experience for starting agentic development from existing work while keeping it isolated and steerable.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent supports auto model selection
item.description:
Copilot cloud agent now supports Copilot auto model selection. Choosing Auto in the model picker lets Copilot pick the best available model based on system health and model availability.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Introducing Copilot CLI agent and unified sessions view in GitHub Copilot for JetBrains IDEs
item.description:
GitHub Copilot for JetBrains IDEs now includes the Copilot CLI agent and a unified sessions view. The sessions view shows live status for running and queued sessions, and the update also adds an ask… feature.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: New enterprise installation API now in public preview
item.description:
GitHub has introduced a new Enterprise Installation API in public preview for GitHub App developers. The API fills a gap identified from community feedback and lets an app determine whether it is installed in an enterprise.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Start Copilot cloud agent tasks via the REST API
item.description:
Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise users can now start Copilot cloud agent tasks programmatically using a new Agent tasks REST API. The feature is in public preview.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Enterprise Server 3.21 release candidate is available
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise Server 3.21 release candidate is available. The release focuses on deployment efficiency, monitoring, code security, and policy management, with organization custom properties now generally available.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot code review: Comment experience improvements
item.description:
GitHub updated Copilot code review comments in the new pull requests experience to make them easier to scan and act on. The changes are available to all users opted into that experience and include grouped suggestions, severity levels, and an updated comment layout.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: April reports are now available to prepare for usage-based billing
item.description:
GitHub now lets customers download April usage reports to see how Copilot activity maps to AI credits ahead of the new usage-based billing rollout. The billing unit of AI credits is going live on a staged schedule.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: CodeQL 2.25.4 adds Swift 6.3.1 support, improvements to C# and Java, and more
item.description:
CodeQL 2.25.4 adds support for Swift 6.3.1 and includes improvements for C# and Java. The release also contains additional updates not detailed in the provided excerpt.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Synchronous SBOM API deprecated
item.description:
GitHub has deprecated the older synchronous SBOM REST API after releasing a new asynchronous SBOM API. The synchronous API is scheduled for removal on November 13, 2026, giving a six-month migration window.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Cross-org Dependabot access for internal repositories
item.description:
Dependabot can now access internal repositories in other organizations within the same enterprise. This lets Dependabot resolve dependencies hosted in cross-org internal GitHub repositories.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Create repositories on the go with GitHub Mobile
item.description:
GitHub Mobile now supports creating new repositories directly in the app. On iOS, you can start from Home or Profile, tap the + button, and select Create repository.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of Grok Code Fast 1
item.description:
GitHub will deprecate Grok Code Fast 1 across all GitHub Copilot experiences on May 15, 2026. The deprecation applies to Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot code review comment types now in usage metrics API
item.description:
The Copilot usage metrics API now reports Copilot code review suggestions broken down by comment type. This adds a new copilot_suggestions_by_comment_type metric for analyzing code review activity.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Disable commit comments on the user level
item.description:
GitHub now lets personal account owners set commit comments to be enabled or disabled by default for their repositories. This adds a user-level default for commit comment behavior across repos they own.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: More flexible secrets and variables for Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub says Copilot cloud agent now supports more flexible handling of secrets and variables when you delegate tasks to it. The agent runs in a GitHub Actions-backed development environment, and users can pass secrets and variables into that environment.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: CodeQL 2.25.3 adds Swift 6.3 support
item.description:
CodeQL 2.25.3 has been released with support for Swift 6.3. The note does not list any other changes.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Actions concurrency groups now allow larger queues
item.description:
You can now allow multiple jobs or workflow runs to wait in the same GitHub Actions concurrency group instead of being limited to a single pending run. Previously, a concurrency…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of GPT-4.1
item.description:
GitHub will deprecate GPT-4.1 across all Copilot experiences on 2026-06-01. Users are directed to switch to the suggested alternative model.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Claude Sonnet 4 deprecated
item.description:
GitHub deprecated Claude Sonnet 4 across all Copilot experiences on May 6, 2026. The deprecation applies to Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprise Live Migrations is now in public preview
item.description:
Enterprise Live Migrations (ELM) is now in public preview. It lets enterprise administrators migrate repositories from GitHub Enterprise Server (GHES) to GitHub Enterprise Cloud.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Rubber Duck in GitHub Copilot CLI now supports more models
item.description:
Rubber Duck in GitHub Copilot CLI now supports additional model combinations. It can use a Claude-powered critic agent in GPT sessions, with support also extended for Claude-based sessions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Repository rulesets: User bypass and branch renaming
item.description:
GitHub repository rulesets now allow individual users to be added as bypass actors. Organization rulesets also now support renaming branches that they cover.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprise-managed plugins in GitHub Copilot CLI are now in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI now supports enterprise-managed plugins in public preview. Enterprise admins can configure and distribute plugins to Copilot CLI users across the organization.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code, April releases
item.description:
GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code changelog covers VS Code releases v1.116 through v1.119 from April and early May 2026. The post notes that Copilot can now search by meaning.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Search and filter bar for repository security advisories
item.description:
GitHub added a search bar and filters to the Security tab’s repository security advisories list. This lets you search and narrow advisories directly within a repository.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning with GitHub MCP Server is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub secret scanning in the GitHub MCP server is now generally available. It can be used with MCP-compatible AI coding agents or IDEs, including GitHub Copilot CLI.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependency scanning with GitHub MCP Server is in public preview
item.description:
GitHub MCP Server now supports dependency scanning in public preview. It can scan code changes for vulnerable dependencies before you commit or open a pull request.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Code-to-cloud risk visibility with Microsoft Defender for Cloud is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub says the code-to-cloud risk visibility integration with Microsoft Defender for Cloud is now generally available. The release includes follow-up improvements since public preview, bringing artifact and runtime context closer together.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Deprecation notice: code_scanning_upload field will be removed from rate_limit API endpoint
item.description:
GitHub will remove the code_scanning_upload field from the rate_limit REST API endpoint response on May 19, 2026. The notice says the field is being deprecated and will no longer be returned after that date.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of GPT-5.2 and GPT-5.2-Codex
item.description:
GitHub says it will deprecate GPT-5.2 and GPT-5.2-Codex across GitHub Copilot experiences, including Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions. The notice says GPT-5.2-Codex is excepted in Copilot, but no replacement or timeline details are included in the provided text.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio — April update
item.description:
The April 2026 GitHub Copilot update for Visual Studio focuses on agentic workflows. It adds direct cloud agent sessions from the IDE, user-level support for custom agents, and a new Debugger agent for validation.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Student GPT-5.3-Codex removal from model picker
item.description:
GitHub is removing GPT-5.3-Codex from the Copilot Student plan’s model picker starting today. The model is still available when using auto model selection.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent starts 20% faster with Actions custom images
item.description:
Copilot cloud agent startup is now over 20% faster. The improvement comes from optimized runner environments built with GitHub Actions custom images.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot code review will start consuming GitHub Actions minutes on June 1, 2026
item.description:
GitHub Copilot code review will begin consuming GitHub Actions minutes starting June 1, 2026. The change applies to code review usage on pull requests.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Notice about upcoming new format for GitHub App installation tokens
item.description:
GitHub will begin a staged rollout on April 27, 2026 to change the format of newly minted GitHub App installation tokens. The update is intended to improve token performance and will happen over several weeks.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Changes to notification retention and archived repository watches
item.description:
GitHub is changing how long notifications are retained and how watches on archived repositories are handled. The rollout is expected to finish over the next few months.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GPT-5.5 is generally available for GitHub Copilot
item.description:
GPT-5.5 is now generally available in GitHub Copilot. GitHub says early testing showed the model performs best on complex, multi-step agentic coding tasks and real-world problem solving.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Inline agent mode in preview and more in GitHub Copilot for JetBrains IDEs
item.description:
GitHub Copilot for JetBrains IDEs adds inline agent mode in preview, along with improvements to Next Edit Suggestions and new controls for approving terminal commands and file edits. The update also introduces global auto-approve and other related enhancements.

  • Source: GitHub
